Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Regarding Confidential Designations; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Dec-24-2025 Advanced From Jan-29-26 To Jan-26-26 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LYFT, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Regarding Confidential Designations; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Regarding Confidential Designations; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jan-26-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Phil Johnson, Esq. Anna Dubrovsky, Esq. 415-746-1481 Anna Dubrovsky Law Group for Plaintiff.
Katelyn Leeviraphan, Esq. (213) 891-9100 CLARK HILL LLP for Defendant Lyft.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

9:04
At the first calling of the case, the court passes the matter due to Co-counsel Ms Dubrovsky's technical difficulties on Zoom.

9:43am The matter is recalled. Plaintiff's co-counsel is present.
ARGUED. Ms. Leeviraphan states Lyft is aligned with court's TR, shown good cause, confidential internal systems, recoup amount sepent on motion. Mr. Johnson contests and argues. The Court poses question on harm to client and counsel on a protective order. Counsel for Plaintiff presents brief rebuttal. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Lyft's Inc.'s Motion for Protective Order.

Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an order. (see scanned order for details)***

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-06-2025 Set For Trial On Sep-21-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production, Set One, And Form Interrogatories, Set One; And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-21-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Phil Johnson, Esq., of ANNA DUBROVSKY LAW GROUP, INC., Tel:(415) 746-1477, for plaintiff
Katelyn Leeviraphan, Esq., of CLARK HILL LLP., Tel:(213) 891-9100 for defendant

Hearing held. A timely Notice of Contest was received from defendants's counsel regarding the sanctions issued on the tentative ruling. The matter argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to request for production (set one) and form interrogatories (set one) is GRANTED.

The propounded discovery is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP 2017.010.) Defendant's objections based on relevance and overbreadth lack merit. Its objection based on the possibility of filing a motion to compel arbitration also lacks merit. Under CCP 1281.4, the action is stayed "upon motion" of a party seeking arbitration. Here, defendant served the objections relying on CCP 1281.4 even though no motion to compel arbitration was pending. Defendant's position lacks substantial justification. If defendant wanted to take advantage of the stay provision of CCP 1281.4, then it should have filed its motion to compel arbitration prior to the discovery response due date.

Defendant shall provide objection-free verified responses to the discovery by November 14, 2025.

The Court has stricken the sanction.

Order signed.

Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/SKF)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Oct-10-2025 Text Ruling

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Phil Johnson, Esq., of ANNA DUBROVSKY LAW GROUP, INC.,Tel: (415) 746-1477 for plaintiff Michelle Guiral
Katelyn Leeviraphan, esq., of CLARK HILL LLP, Tel:(213) 891-9100 for Defendant Lyft, Inc
Court Reporter: Irene Nakamura, CSR # 9478, Email: iny@ideporeporters.com, Tel: (323) 393-3768.

Hearing held. A timely Notice of Contest was received from defendant's counsel regarding Court's tentative ruling "the conclusion that the California Arbitration Act applies and the existence of multiple parties results in a possibility of conflicting rulings of law or fact." The matter argued/discussed. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT LYFT, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is taken under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang;Court Reporter: Irene Nakamura, CSR # 9478, Email: iny@ideporeporters.com, Tel: (323) 393-3768. Reported. (302/JMQ)
